description: Completely rewrite generate_excel.py to create a MiUHC Plans 1-5 premium calculator Excel file with hardcoded premium data, calculator-style input interface, INDEX/MATCH lookup formulas, data validation dropdowns, and two sheets (Konvensional and Syariah).
steps: STEP 1 - Remove all existing PDF parsing code and old plan structure (Diamond/Ruby/Emerald etc). Keep and enhance the XlsxWriter class which is needed to build xlsx from scratch. The new script will use ONLY hardcoded premium data.

STEP 3 - Enhance XlsxWriter class if needed. The existing class already supports: shared strings, formula cells, number cells, data validation, merged cells, hyperlinks, styles (bold, green bg white text, bold green text, right-aligned, number format #,##0). Additional style needed: a style for input cells (perhaps light yellow background or bordered cells to highlight user-editable cells). Also add support for date cells if needed for Tanggal Lahir field, or just use a text input.

STEP 4 - Build the calculator interface layout for each sheet. The layout should be:
  Row 1: Header 'PETRUS JAKUB MANULIFE 087781896087' merged across columns, with hyperlink to https://wa.me/6287781896087
  Row 2: Subtitle - for Konvensional: 'MiUHC Konvensional - Efektif 1 September 2025', for Syariah: 'MiUHC Syariah - Efektif 1 Januari 2024'
  Row 3: Empty
  Row 4: Label 'Nama Tertanggung:' in A4, input cell B4 (empty, user types name)
  Row 5: Label 'Jenis Kelamin:' in A5, input cell B5 with dropdown validation (Pria, Wanita)
  Row 6: Label 'Tanggal Lahir:' in A6, input cell B6 (date format or text)
  Row 7: Label 'Usia:' in A7, formula in B7 that calculates age from B6 (use DATEDIF or YEAR formula: =DATEDIF(B6,TODAY(),"Y") or =INT((TODAY()-B6)/365.25))
  Row 8: Label 'Plan:' in A8, input cell B8 with dropdown validation ('Plan 1','Plan 2','Plan 3','Plan 4','Plan 5')
  Row 9: Empty
  Row 10: Label 'HASIL PERHITUNGAN' (bold, section header)
  Row 11: Label 'Premi Tahunan:' in A11, formula in B11 that looks up annual premium from data table based on Gender (B5), Age Band (derived from B7), and Plan (B8)
  Row 12: Label 'Premi Bulanan:' in A12, formula in B12 = B11/10
  (Adjust row numbers as needed for clean layout with spacing)

STEP 5 - Build the premium data reference table below the calculator interface (hidden area or visible reference). This table stores all 50 premium values (5 plans x 5 age bands x 2 genders). The table structure should support INDEX/MATCH or VLOOKUP formulas. Suggested layout starting around row 16:
  Reference table with columns: Key (e.g. 'Pria_20-30'), Plan 1, Plan 2, Plan 3, Plan 4, Plan 5
  10 rows: Pria_20-30, Pria_31-40, Pria_41-50, Pria_51-60, Pria_61-70, Wanita_20-30, Wanita_31-40, Wanita_41-50, Wanita_51-60, Wanita_61-70

STEP 6 - Implement the lookup formula for Premi Tahunan. The formula needs to:
  1. Determine the age band from the age in B7 (use nested IF or LOOKUP: IF(B7<=30,'20-30',IF(B7<=40,'31-40',IF(B7<=50,'41-50',IF(B7<=60,'51-60','61-70')))))
  2. Concatenate gender + age band to form the lookup key
  3. Use INDEX/MATCH to find the premium: =IFERROR(INDEX(data_range, MATCH(B5&"_"&age_band_formula, key_column, 0), MATCH(B8, plan_header_row, 0)), "")
  The formula can be complex - that is fine for Excel.

STEP 7 - Create both sheets with identical data but different titles/dates:
  Sheet 1: 'Konvensional' with subtitle 'MiUHC Konvensional - Efektif 1 September 2025'
  Sheet 2: 'Syariah' with subtitle 'MiUHC Syariah - Efektif 1 Januari 2024'
  Both sheets use the same premium data table and same calculator layout.
STEP 8 - Set output filename to 'Kalkulator_Premi_MiUHC.xlsx' (renamed from old 'Tabel_Premi_MiUHC.xlsx'). Update the OUTPUT_XLSX variable. Remove any reference to PDF files.

STEP 9 - Add number formatting for premium result cells using Indonesian Rupiah style: 'Rp #.##0' or just '#,##0' with the Rp prefix in the label. The existing numFmt 164 uses '#,##0' which is fine for the numbers. Add 'Rp ' text prefix to the label cells (e.g. 'Premi Tahunan (Rp):') or use a custom number format.
STEP 10 - Run the script and verify output. The script should generate the xlsx without any errors and without requiring any PDF files or external dependencies.
acceptance_criteria: Running 'python3 /projects/sandbox/kirodev/generate_excel.py' completes without errors and without needing any PDF files
Output file /projects/sandbox/kirodev/Kalkulator_Premi_MiUHC.xlsx exists and is a valid ZIP/xlsx file
The xlsx contains exactly two sheets named 'Konvensional' and 'Syariah'
Both sheets have the header 'PETRUS JAKUB MANULIFE 087781896087' with hyperlink to https://wa.me/6287781896087
Both sheets have input fields: Nama Tertanggung, Jenis Kelamin (dropdown: Pria/Wanita), Tanggal Lahir, Usia (formula), Plan (dropdown: Plan 1-5)
Both sheets have result formulas: Premi Tahunan (lookup), Premi Bulanan (Tahunan/10)
Premium data reference table with 50 values (5 plans x 5 age bands x 2 genders) is present in each sheet
Lookup formulas use INDEX/MATCH or equivalent to find premium based on gender + age band + plan
Number format uses #,##0 (thousands separator) for premium values
No PDF parsing code remains in the script
Script uses only Python standard library (no pip packages)
verification: python3 -c "import zipfile; z=zipfile.ZipFile('/projects/sandbox/kirodev/Kalkulator_Premi_MiUHC.xlsx'); print('Valid xlsx:', z.namelist()); z.close()"

python3 -c "import zipfile, xml.etree.ElementTree as ET; z=zipfile.ZipFile('/projects/sandbox/kirodev/Kalkulator_Premi_MiUHC.xlsx'); wb=ET.parse(z.open('xl/workbook.xml')); ns={'main':'http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/spreadsheetml/2006/main'}; sheets=wb.findall('.//main:sheet',ns); print('Sheets:', [s.get('name') for s in sheets]); z.close()"

python3 -c "import zipfile, xml.etree.ElementTree as ET; z=zipfile.ZipFile('/projects/sandbox/kirodev/Kalkulator_Premi_MiUHC.xlsx'); ss=ET.parse(z.open('xl/sharedStrings.xml')); ns={'main':'http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/spreadsheetml/2006/main'}; texts=[t.text for t in ss.getroot().iter('{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/spreadsheetml/2006/main}t') if t.text]; print('Header found:', any('PETRUS JAKUB' in t for t in texts)); print('Plan 1 found:', any('Plan 1' in t for t in texts)); print('Plan 5 found:', any('Plan 5' in t for t in texts)); z.close()"

python3 -c "import zipfile, xml.etree.ElementTree as ET; z=zipfile.ZipFile('/projects/sandbox/kirodev/Kalkulator_Premi_MiUHC.xlsx'); s1=ET.parse(z.open('xl/worksheets/sheet1.xml')); ns={'main':'http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/spreadsheetml/2006/main'}; formulas=s1.getroot().findall('.//{http://schemas.openxmlformats.org/spreadsheetml/2006/main}f'); print('Formula count:', len(formulas)); print('Sample formulas:', [f.text[:60] for f in formulas[:3] if f.text]); z.close()"
findings: Implementation complete. All verification checks pass: valid xlsx with 2 sheets (Konvensional, Syariah), correct shared strings, 3 formulas per sheet (age calc, INDEX/MATCH lookup, monthly premium), 2 data validations per sheet (gender and plan dropdowns), hyperlink to WhatsApp, and all 50 premium values correctly placed in data tables.
